methodsAttempted.query: site:uef.fi University of Eastern Finland artificial intelligence guidelines students teachers generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned the official UEF Kamu AI policy page, a public Kamu guideline-scope accordion page, a UEF research news article, and UEF repository PDFs.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uef.fi/kamu.fi tekoäly opiskelijat opetus tutkimus University of Eastern Finland
methodsAttempted.notes: A Finnish-language official-source query did not return a public Finnish policy page through Firecrawl search.

methodsAttempted.query: Guessed Finnish Kamu path for the English AI policy page
methodsAttempted.notes: The guessed Finnish URL returned HTTP 404 and was rejected as stale or unavailable.
Robots.txt was checked and returned a site-wide Crawl-Delay directive. The two public English Kamu pages were fetched with HTTP and Firecrawl without bypassing access controls.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found two public UEF Kamu pages with directly usable AI policy and guideline evidence. The full AI policy PDF was available only through a UEF SharePoint/intranet location, a guessed Finnish public path returned 404, and research/news outputs were rejected as non-policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: University of Eastern Finland states that its AI guidelines apply as a general guideline for the university's use of these services in research and teaching/studies, while faculties may provide specifying instructions within the general guidelines and legislation.

citation.citationTitle: Definition of artificial intelligence and application of the guidelines - UEF Kamu
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Eastern Finland says students may use AI to support learning and study attainments unless a teacher forbids it, but use of a generative AI application or similar in preparing study attainments must always be mentioned.

citation.citationTitle: AI POLICY FOR STUDENTS AT THE UNIVERSITY OF EASTERN FINLAND - UEF Kamu
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Eastern Finland says use of AI against a teacher's instructions, or failing to report AI use, is treated as fraudulent conduct and investigated through the university's fraud process.

citation.citationTitle: AI POLICY FOR STUDENTS AT THE UNIVERSITY OF EASTERN FINLAND - UEF Kamu
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Eastern Finland offers Microsoft Copilot Enterprise for students over 18 via UEF login, and tells students not to use a UEF email address or university-network credential combination when logging in to external AI applications.

artifacts.claimText: University of Eastern Finland says material containing personal data must not be exported to AI applications unless the university has an agreement covering personal-data processing, and that confidential information must not be entered into external AI applications.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI POLICY FOR STUDENTS AT THE UNIVERSITY OF EASTERN FINLAND - UEF Kamu
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If a student uses an AI application during a course or as part of a study attainment (e.g., an exam, assignment) where the use of AI has been forbidden in advance, the student's activities are deemed to be fraudulent and will be treated in the same way as other cases of fraudulent conduct. The same applies to situations where the student does not report the use of AI.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim because the public UEF Kamu page directly states general guideline scope and faculty-level specification limits.
Approved as a narrow student-facing academic-integrity claim supported by the public UEF Kamu guidelines-in-brief text.
Approved because the public page explicitly links forbidden AI use and non-disclosure to fraudulent conduct and the fraud process.
Approved as a narrow AI-tool-treatment claim; it does not state exclusive approval of Copilot and preserves the external-account restriction.
Approved as a privacy claim limited to personal-data export and confidential-information handling in AI applications.
Approved as a narrow thesis-use claim supported by the public UEF Kamu thesis section.

artifacts.title: University of Eastern Finland AI Policy Evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence from public official UEF Kamu pages supports narrow claims that the University of Eastern Finland applies AI guidelines as general guidance for teaching/studies and research use, allows student AI support unless forbidden with disclosure required, treats forbidden or undisclosed AI use through the fraud process, offers Copilot Enterprise via UEF login, restricts external AI account credential use, restricts personal and confidential data in AI applications, and requires thesis disclosure plus independent work.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The full UEF AI policy was linked from the public page to a SharePoint/intranet location and was not accessed.
Claims are staged candidates only and were not marked human reviewed or canonical.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
